recommendations.name: OEM-Spec Spark Plug Replacement (Iridium)
recommendations.description: At 180,000 miles, spark plugs are almost certainly due for replacement. Swap to NGK Iridium IX or Denso Iridium Power plugs for the 2AR-FE or 2GR-FE engine. Iridium tips provide better ignition efficiency, improved fuel economy, and longer service life than standard copper plugs. This is the single most impactful tune-up item for a high-mileage Camry.

recommendations.name: Serpentine Belt & Tensioner Replacement
recommendations.description: The serpentine belt drives the alternator, power steering pump, and A/C compressor. At this mileage, the belt and tensioner spring are worn and at risk of snapping — leaving you stranded. Replace both as a set. Gates and Dayco make direct-fit OEM-quality kits for the 2012 Camry.

recommendations.name: Engine Air Filter Upgrade (K&N Drop-In)
recommendations.description: A K&N High-Flow drop-in replacement filter improves airflow over the stock paper filter and is cleanable/reusable for up to 50,000 miles. For a daily driver at 180k, it ensures the engine breathes clean air without the commitment of a full cold-air intake. Simply swaps into the factory airbox — no tools needed.

recommendations.name: Transmission Fluid Flush & Upgraded Fluid
recommendations.description: The U660E/U760E automatic transmission fluid is often never changed by previous owners. A full drain-and-fill with fresh Toyota WS-compatible synthetic ATF dramatically reduces wear on clutch packs and valve body internals. At 180k, this is critical for transmission longevity. Do 2–3 partial drain-and-fills if a flush machine isn't available.

recommendations.name: Coolant Flush & Mishimoto Radiator Cap Upgrade
recommendations.description: Old coolant loses its anti-corrosion and anti-boil properties over time. Flush and refill with Toyota Super Long Life Coolant (pink/red). Pair with a Mishimoto billet aluminum radiator cap for a secure, pressure-rated seal that prevents coolant loss under heat cycling — a common issue on aging 2012 Camrys.
brand_tips: Toyota Genuine Super Long Life Coolant (pink)

recommendations.name: OEM-Quality Timing Cover & Valve Cover Gasket Inspection/Replacement
recommendations.description: At 180,000 miles, rubber valve cover gaskets on the 2AR-FE/2GR-FE are prone to oil seeping onto exhaust manifolds — a fire and smoke risk. Inspect and replace with Fel-Pro or Toyota OEM gaskets. This is a moderate DIY job but well within reach for a beginner with basic hand tools and a YouTube guide.
brand_tips: Fel-Pro VS 50638 R Valve Cover Gasket Set

recommendations.name: Synthetic Engine Oil Conversion (Full Synthetic 0W-20)
recommendations.description: If not already on full synthetic, switch the 2012 Camry to a 0W-20 full synthetic oil on the next oil change. At high mileage, consider a high-mileage formula with seal conditioners to reduce minor seepage. This reduces cold-start wear significantly and extends engine life at 180k+ miles.

recommendations.name: Fuel System Cleaner & Injector Service
recommendations.description: After 180k miles, fuel injectors and intake valves accumulate carbon deposits that hurt fuel economy and idle quality. Use a concentrated fuel system cleaner through the tank for mild buildup, or have a professional run an injector cleaning kit through the fuel rail. Chevron Techron is well-regarded for Toyota engines.

recommendations.name: Brake Fluid Flush (DOT 3/4 Replacement)
recommendations.description: Brake fluid is hygroscopic — it absorbs moisture over time, lowering its boiling point and creating a spongy pedal. After years of use on a high-mileage daily driver, a full brake fluid flush with fresh DOT 3 or DOT 4 fluid restores braking confidence and protects ABS module internals from corrosion.

recommendations.name: Battery & Charging System Test + Battery Replacement
recommendations.description: A 2012 Camry battery is likely original or near end-of-life. Test the battery and alternator output with a multimeter or free test at AutoZone/O'Reilly. If voltage is below spec, replace with an Optima or OdysseyGroup 35 AGM battery for superior cold-crank amps, vibration resistance, and longer service life on a daily driver.
